top_signals.title: Intense discounting pushes food inflation to year low
top_signals.summary: Intense competition among UK supermarkets has pushed food inflation to its lowest level in a year, despite warnings that the Iran war will push up prices. Heavy discounting saw food inflation fall from 3.1 per cent last month to 2.7 per cent in May, according to data by the British Retail Consortium (BRC) and NIQ. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: CNBC's The China Connection newsletter: Tariffs eased. Trust didn't.
top_signals.summary: As the largest consumer market globally, the U.S. still remains a prized destination for Chinese companies that are finding ways to address local concerns.

top_signals.title: Nuclear verdict alert: almost $50M against a mystery Texas trucking company
top_signals.summary:
A $50 million nuclear verdict was handed down against a Texas trucking company that may not be in business.

The post Nuclear verdict alert: almost $50M against a mystery Texas trucking company appeared first on FreightWaves.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Labour has not delivered on planning reform, manufacturers say
top_signals.summary: UK manufacturers have hit out at the country’s archaic planning system, claiming the Labour government has not delivered on stated aims to back builders over blockers. A new report from industry body Make UK found that almost half of manufacturers (46 per cent) believe the current planning system is still hampering the UK’s economic potential, [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: The Rest is investing: Gary Lineker-backed Goalhanger launches venture capital arm
top_signals.summary: The UK’s biggest independent podcast group is launching its own venture capital arm, City AM can reveal, as it reinvests the fruits of its success by building stakes in other media startups. The Kennington-based business, which was co-founded by former BBC presenter Gary Lineker and produces The Rest is History, The Rest is Entertainment and [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
